pool_brief: You are given a starter HTML file for a Nigerian business scenario. Write script.js to process and log the page data using variables, data types, and operators. Your script.js must: (1) declare at least 5 variables — use const for values that will not change, let for at least one value that you reassign; (2) use all three value types — string, number, and boolean — at least once each; (3) perform at least two arithmetic calculations using different operators (include % at least once); (4) include at least two comparisons using === and at least one logical operator (&& or || or !); (5) log every output with a descriptive label using console.log('Label:', value). Do not modify the HTML file.

project_pool.context: An e-commerce cart page for a Jumia Nigeria order with multiple items, a coupon, and VAT.

files.content:
Jumia Nigeria

Nigeria's No. 1 Online Shopping Destination

My Cart

  • Tecno Spark 20 Pro ₦89,000 Qty: 1
  • Wireless Earbuds (JBL) ₦12,500 Qty: 2

Order Summary

Phone: ₦89,000 × 1

Earbuds: ₦12,500 × 2

Coupon code: JUMIA20 (₦2,000 off)

VAT: 7.5%

Free delivery on orders above ₦50,000

Premium member: No

criteria.description: At least 5 variables declared: phone price (89000), earbuds price (12500), earbuds quantity (2), coupon discount (2000), VAT rate (0.075), and at least one let variable that is reassigned (e.g. a running total or a status string that changes). const is used for all fixed values; let only where reassignment occurs

All three value types are used (strings for labels or store name, numbers for prices/totals, booleans for comparisons); arithmetic uses at least two operators including % (e.g. to check if total is a multiple of 1000, or to check if earbud count is even); at least two comparisons use ===; at least one logical operator (&&, ||, or !) is present

All logged values are arithmetically correct: subtotal = 89000 + (12500 * 2) = 114000; after coupon = 112000; VAT = 8400; grandTotal = 120400; free delivery check logs true (114000 > 50000); all logs have descriptive labels

camelCase naming throughout; no var; const/let used appropriately (computed totals are const once calculated); consistent indentation; no bare magic numbers in calculations — all prices come from named variables

project_pool.context: A personal savings dashboard for a PiggyVest user tracking their monthly savings goal progress.

files.content:
PiggyVest

Save better. Invest smarter.

Monthly Savings Goal

Emergency Fund

Target: ₦150,000

Saved so far: ₦87,500

Months remaining: 3

Account Details

Ada Okonkwo

Plan: Premium

Current saving streak: 14 days

Daily savings rate: ₦1,500

criteria.description: At least 5 variables declared from the page data: target (150000), saved (87500), months remaining (3), daily rate (1500), streak (14), and at least one let variable that is reassigned (e.g. a projected savings amount that updates, or a status string that changes). const used for fixed values

All three value types used (string for user name or app name, numbers for financial amounts, booleans from comparisons); arithmetic uses at least two operators including % (e.g. to check if streak is a multiple of 7 — a full week — or if savings amount is divisible by 1000); at least two === comparisons; at least one logical operator

All calculated values are correct: remaining = 150000 - 87500 = 62500; projected = daily rate * 30 * months = 135000; goal achievable check (projected >= remaining) logs true (135000 >= 62500); streak milestone check uses % correctly; all logs have descriptive labels
camelCase naming; no var; const for all computed results that won't change; let only for reassigned values; descriptive labels on all console.log calls; no bare magic numbers in calculations

project_pool.context: A ride summary page for a Bolt trip in Lagos showing distance, fare breakdown, and driver rating.

files.content:
Bolt

Your city. Your ride.

Trip Details

Victoria Island to Lekki Phase 1, Lagos

Distance: 9.4 km

Duration: 22 minutes

Fare Breakdown

Base fare: ₦400

Rate per km: ₦120

Booking fee: ₦50

Promo applied: ₦200 off (first ride)

Tip: optional, 0 by default

Driver Info

Driver: Emeka O.

Rating: 4.92

Trips completed: 843

criteria.description: At least 5 variables from the page: baseFare (400), ratePerKm (120), distance (9.4), bookingFee (50), promoDiscount (200), and at least one let variable (e.g. tip, starting at 0 and reassigned to a non-zero amount). const for all fixed values

All three value types present (string for route or driver name, numbers for fares and distance, booleans from comparisons); arithmetic uses multiplication and addition/subtraction at minimum, plus % for at least one check (e.g. whether trip count is a multiple of 100, or whether fare rounded to nearest 50 leaves a remainder); at least two === comparisons; at least one logical operator

Core calculation is correct: distanceFare = 9.4 * 120 = 1128; subTotal = 400 + 1128 + 50 = 1578; afterPromo = 1578 - 200 = 1378; all comparisons produce correct booleans; all logs have descriptive labels
camelCase naming; no var; const/let used correctly; tip is let and gets reassigned; all logs have clear descriptive labels; no magic numbers used directly in calculations

project_pool.context: A payment confirmation page for a Flutterwave transaction showing amount, fees, exchange rate, and account status.

files.content:
Flutterwave

Powering payments across Africa

Transaction Details

Merchant: Publica Academy

Reference: FLW-2025-0044821

Amount: ₦25,000

Processing fee: 1.4% (capped at ₦2,000)

Currency: NGN

Account Details

Bayo Adeyemi

Account: 0123456789

Bank: GTBank

KYC verified: Yes

Daily transfer limit: ₦500,000

Transfers today: 3

criteria.description: At least 5 variables from the page: amount (25000), feeRate (0.014), feeCap (2000), dailyLimit (500000), transfersToday (3), isVerified (true), and at least one let variable that is reassigned (e.g. a running total or a status variable). const for fixed values

All three value types present (strings for merchant name or reference, numbers for amounts and fees, booleans from comparisons); arithmetic uses multiplication for fee calculation and at least one other operator; % used for at least one check (e.g. whether transfers today is a multiple of 3, or whether amount is divisible by 5000); at least two === comparisons; at least one logical operator

Fee calculation is correct: rawFee = 25000 * 0.014 = 350; since 350 < 2000 cap, processingFee = 350; totalCharged = 25000 + 350 = 25350; all comparisons produce correct booleans; all logs have descriptive labels
camelCase naming; no var; const for all values that don't change after calculation; let only for reassigned values; all console.log calls use descriptive labels; no bare magic numbers in calculations

project_pool.context: A monthly budget summary page for a Cowry Budget app user tracking income, expenses, and savings in Naira.

files.content:
Cowry Budget

Track every Naira. Build every dream.

April 2025 — Monthly Summary

User: Ngozi Eze

Total income: ₦285,000

Rent: ₦80,000

Food & groceries: ₦35,000

Transport: ₦18,500

Utilities & bills: ₦12,000

Entertainment: ₦9,500

Monthly savings goal: ₦60,000

Days in April: 30

criteria.description: At least 5 variables from the page: income (285000), rent (80000), food (35000), transport (18500), utilities (12000), entertainment (9500), savingsGoal (60000), and at least one let variable that is reassigned (e.g. a running total of expenses that grows, or a status string updated after the savings check). const for all fixed values

All three value types present (strings for username or category labels, numbers for all financial values, booleans from comparisons); arithmetic uses addition for total expenses and subtraction for remaining balance at minimum; % used for at least one check (e.g. whether the savings amount is divisible by a round number, or whether the number of expense categories is even); at least two === comparisons; at least one logical operator

Core calculations are correct: totalExpenses = 80000 + 35000 + 18500 + 12000 + 9500 = 155000; remaining = 285000 - 155000 = 130000; goalMet check (remaining >= savingsGoal) logs true (130000 >= 60000); all logs have descriptive labels

camelCase naming; no var; const/let used correctly; all console.log calls include descriptive labels with currency symbol where appropriate; no bare magic numbers in calculations — all values come from named variables
